incorporations.reason: Standardises the translation of 'Patria' to 'Fatherland' throughout, fixing the inconsistency flagged in the Claude evaluation

incorporations.original: the brigands who trafficked in their Patria
incorporations.replacement: the brigands who trafficked in their Fatherland
incorporations.reason: Same Patria/Fatherland consistency fix as above

incorporations.original: Miss Elisa contrived once more to invoke the mysterious stranger
incorporations.replacement: Miss Elisa brought up once more the mysterious stranger
incorporations.reason: 'brought up' is a more natural rendering of 'tirò ancora in ballo' (literally 'dragged into the dance again'); 'contrived to invoke' over-translates

incorporations.original: The mysterious stranger was herself. It was Elisa who provided out of her own savings
incorporations.replacement: The mysterious stranger is there, in despair. It was Elisa who provided out of her own savings
incorporations.reason: Corrects a factual mistranslation. Italian 'Lo straniero misterioso è là che si dispera' means 'The mysterious stranger is there, despairing' — Sara is pointing toward Elisa in the other room. Claude's 'was herself' invents an identification not in the source text.

incorporations.original: The Count di Rudio turned the great poet over and over in his hands
incorporations.replacement: The Count di Rudio gripped the great poet, turned him over and over in his hands
incorporations.reason: Restores the verb 'strinse' (gripped/squeezed) which Claude omitted. The Italian has four actions — strinse, girò e rigirò e capovolse — and the grip conveys di Rudio's emotional agitation. Gemini had 'squeezed'; 'gripped' better suits the physical gesture with a book.

incorporations.original: Making his way home toward midnight
incorporations.replacement: Making his way home toward one o'clock
incorporations.reason: Corrects a factual error. Italian 'verso il tocco' means toward one o'clock (the single bell-stroke marking the first hour), not midnight.

incorporations.original: like the ancient Roman women of old
incorporations.reason: Italian 'come le sue antiche romane' — 'sue' (his) is significant: Elisa means the Roman women of his heritage. Claude's 'of old' is redundant with 'ancient' and loses the possessive.

incorporations.original: The love he bore his Patria did not make her jealous
incorporations.replacement: The love he bore his Fatherland did not make her jealous
incorporations.reason: Same Patria/Fatherland consistency fix applied throughout Elisa's speech
